task: Write a Python script that reads a CSV file, analyzes each column (numeric: mean, median, std, min, max; categorical: unique values, mode, frequency), and generates a summary report as a formatted text file. Use only the standard library. Handle missing values, mixed types, and large files via chunking. Test with a sample CSV.
description: Build a CSV data analyzer with standard library
expected_output: A CSV analyzer script that outputs a formatted summary report

task: Create a web scraper in Python using requests and BeautifulSoup4. Scrape a documentation site (e.g., http://example.com or any public site) and extract all headings (h1-h6), links (href), and code blocks. Save the output as structured JSON. Include the URL, timestamp, and page title. Handle HTTP errors, timeouts, and relative URLs.
description: Build a web scraper with requests and BeautifulSoup
expected_output: A web scraper that saves structured data as JSON

task: Write a Python CLI task manager. Support commands: add, list, done, delete, and clear. Store tasks in a JSON file. Each task has id, description, created_at, done (bool), and priority (high/medium/low). Support filtering (--done, --priority) and sorting (--sort created/priority). Use argparse for CLI args. Color-code output with ANSI.
description: Build a CLI task manager with JSON storage
expected_output: A CLI task manager app with full CRUD

task: Create a file organizer Python script that scans a directory and organizes files into subdirectories by type (Images/, Documents/, Code/, Archives/, Audio/, Video/, Others/) based on file extension mappings. Handle nested directories. Support dry-run mode (--dry-run). Log all moves to a file. Handle filename conflicts by appending a number.
description: Build a file organizer by extension type
expected_output: A file organizer script that sorts files into category folders

task: Write a Python class that implements a progress bar decorator/context manager. Support: custom message, total count, bar width (default 40), fill character, and ETA calculation. Show percentage, current/total, elapsed time, and items/second. Auto-detect terminal width. Work in Jupyter notebooks (use IPython display). Use only standard library.
description: Build a progress bar with ETA and auto-width
expected_output: A reusable ProgressBar class with decorator and context manager

task: Build a lightweight HTTP server in Python using only the standard library (http.server module or sockets). Support GET and POST methods. Serve static files from a directory. Parse URL query parameters. Handle 404, 403, and 500 errors. Add a simple routing system. Log requests to stdout. Make it concurrent with threading.
description: Build an HTTP server with routing using only stdlib
expected_output: A functional HTTP server with routing and concurrency

task: Create a password generator CLI tool in Python. Support: length (default 16), --uppercase, --lowercase, --digits, --symbols flags, --exclude to exclude certain chars, --count to generate multiple passwords, and --no-ambiguous to exclude chars like il1Lo0O. Evaluate entropy of generated passwords. Output as a table. Use argparse and secrets module.
description: Build a secure password generator with entropy evaluation
expected_output: A CLI password generator with multiple options

task: Write a Python log file parser that reads server access logs (Apache/Nginx combined format). Parse each line into structured fields: IP, timestamp, method, path, status, size, user-agent, referer. Generate statistics: top 10 IPs, top 10 paths, status code distribution, hourly request count, and 404s. Output as both formatted text and JSON.
description: Build a log file parser for Apache/Nginx access logs
expected_output: A log parser that produces statistics in text and JSON

task: Create a Python script that recursively searches text files for a regex pattern (like grep). Support: --ignore-case, --count (show match count per file), --include (file glob), --exclude-dir, --context (lines around match), --color (highlight matches). Handle binary files gracefully (skip with warning). Output in format: filename:line:content. Use only stdlib.
description: Build a grep-like text search tool
expected_output: A grep-like search tool with all specified options
eval_criteria: all flags implemented (ignore-case, count, include, exclude-dir, context, color)

task: Write a Python data backup script that: 1) Compresses a specified directory into a tar.gz archive, 2) Splits large archives (>100MB) into chunks, 3) Optionally encrypts with a password (using cryptography library), 4) Cleanup old backups (keep last N), 5) Logs all operations with timestamps. Support config via a YAML or JSON file. Add --dry-run.
description: Build a backup script with compression, splitting, and encryption
expected_output: A backup script with all specified features

task: Build a Markdown to HTML converter in Python using only standard library. Support: headings (h1-h6), bold/italic, links, images, code blocks (with language), inline code, ordered/unordered lists, blockquotes, and horizontal rules. Output valid HTML5. Handle nested formatting. Use regex-based parsing. Test with sample markdown.
description: Build a Markdown-to-HTML converter with stdlib
expected_output: A markdown to HTML converter supporting all common elements

task: Create a Python system monitor script that displays: CPU usage per core, memory usage (total/used/available/percent), disk usage (all mounted partitions), network I/O (bytes sent/received), and top 5 processes by CPU. Use psutil library. Refresh every 2 seconds. Display in a terminal dashboard using curses. Color-code warnings (>80% usage = red).
description: Build a terminal dashboard system monitor with curses
expected_output: A real-time terminal dashboard showing system metrics

task: Write a Python script that validates and beautifies JSON. Read JSON from stdin or a file. Validate structure and report errors with line/column. On valid JSON, pretty-print with configurable indent (default 2), sort keys, and optional strip. Support streaming for large arrays. Add --compact for minified output. Handle non-ASCII characters properly.
description: Build a JSON validator and beautifier
expected_output: A JSON validator and beautifier CLI tool

task: Create a simple unit test framework in Python (like a minimal pytest). Support: test discovery (functions starting with test_), assertions (eq, ne, gt, lt, is_true, is_false, raises), setup/teardown per test and per module, test fixtures via decorator, test filtering by pattern, and output with pass/fail counts and timing. Use only stdlib.
description: Build a minimal unit test framework
expected_output: A minimal but functional test framework

task: Build a simple message queue system in Python. Implement: create_queue(name), publish(queue_name, message), subscribe(queue_name, callback), ack(message_id), and purge(queue_name). Support at-least-once delivery with acknowledgment. Store pending messages in memory or SQLite. Handle callback failures with retry (3 attempts) then dead-letter queue. Async support optional.
description: Build a simple message queue system with retry and DLQ
expected_output: A working message queue with at-least-once delivery
